title: Bill on Clean Transportation Standard Program Introduced in Minnesota House
content: On March 26 2025, Rep. Erin Koegel (D) and 3 other Democrat cosponsors introduced HF 2847 on a Clean Transportation Fuel Standard Program in the House of Representatives. The Bill states that " It is the goal of the state to reduce the aggregate carbon intensity of transportation fuel supplied to Minnesota to net zero life cycle greenhouse gas emissions on and after 2050." It aims to decrease the aggregate carbon intensity of transportation fuel by at least 25 percent below the baseline level (the baseline year being 2018) ten years after the year of enactment and 75 percent below the baseline level after 20 years of enactment of the standard. It has been referred to the Transportation Finance and Policy Committee.
author: Analysts Account
